headline: Children in conflict with law held 47 days average before hearing
context: Children arrested and charged in Malaysia spent an average of 47 days in remand before their first court hearing in 2024.

cards.big: Children spend average 47 days in remand before first court hearing
cards.sub: Child Act 2001 mandates hearings without unreasonable delay

cards.big: Child Act 2001 Section 84 requires cases to be disposed of expeditiously
cards.sub: No statutory maximum period defined for pretrial detention of children

cards.big: CRC requires detention of minors only as last resort and shortest period
cards.sub: 47-day average pretrial detention contradicts international standards

cards.big: 1,340 children were arrested and charged in Malaysia in 2024
cards.sub: 67% came from families below the poverty line per welfare department records

cards.big: Detaining children 47 days before even hearing their case is not child justice
UNICEF Malaysia recommended diversion programs as alternative to prosecution
